skill.name: Microsoft 365 Agents SDK (Python)
skill.description: Build multichannel agents for Microsoft Teams, M365, and Copilot Studio using the Microsoft Agents SDK with aiohttp hosting and MSAL authentication.
skill.summary: Create enterprise-grade AI agents that integrate with Microsoft 365 ecosystem including Teams, Copilot Studio, and Graph API.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only instructional skill that provides code examples for building Microsoft 365 agents. Static analysis detected no security issues. The skill contains legitimate SDK usage patterns for aiohttp hosting, MSAL authentication, and Copilot Studio integration. Environment variable usage follows security best practices.

content.user_title: Build Microsoft 365 AI Agents with Python
content.value_statement: Organizations need AI agents that integrate seamlessly with Microsoft 365. This skill provides production-ready patterns for building Teams bots and Copilot Studio agents using the official Microsoft Agents SDK.
actual_capabilities: Generate aiohttp-hosted agent applications with proper request routing
Implement AgentApplication message handlers with regex and keyword matching
Configure MSAL-based OAuth authentication for Microsoft Graph access
Set up streaming responses with Azure OpenAI integration
Create Copilot Studio client connections for direct agent communication
Configure environment variables for secure credential management
limitations: Requires valid Microsoft Azure and M365 tenant configuration
OAuth flows require registered Azure AD applications with proper permissions
Production deployments need persistent storage instead of MemoryStorage
Copilot Studio integration requires Power Platform environment access

use_cases.title: Enterprise Teams Bot Development
use_cases.description: Build internal company bots for Teams that handle HR queries, IT support tickets, or onboarding workflows with Graph API integration.
use_cases.target_user: Enterprise developer building Microsoft Teams applications

use_cases.title: Copilot Studio Agent Integration
use_cases.description: Create custom AI agents that connect to Copilot Studio engines for specialized domain knowledge and enterprise data access.
use_cases.target_user: Solution architect designing conversational AI experiences

use_cases.title: Multi-Channel Agent Deployment
use_cases.description: Deploy a single agent application that serves multiple channels including Teams, web chat, and custom clients through standardized routing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Agent Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Microsoft 365 agent with aiohttp hosting that responds to 'hello' and 'help' messages. Include basic conversation update handlers and error handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Setting up your first M365 agent

prompt_templates.title: OAuth-Protected Message Handler
prompt_templates.prompt: Add an auth-protected route that retrieves the user's Microsoft Graph profile and displays their name. Use MSAL connection manager for token acquisition.

prompt_templates.title: Streaming Response with Azure OpenAI
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement a streaming response handler that uses Azure OpenAI to generate creative content. Include sensitivity labels and feedback loop configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Copilot Studio Client Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a standalone script that connects to Copilot Studio using the client SDK. Include token acquisition and conversation management.

output_examples.input: Set up a basic Teams bot that echoes user messages
output: Agent application initialized with aiohttp CloudAdapter
Message handler registered for activity type: message
Conversation update handler registered for: membersAdded
Server running on localhost:3978 with JWT authorization middleware

output_examples.input: Add OAuth authentication for Microsoft Graph access
output: MsalConnectionManager configured with Azure AD settings
Auth handler 'GRAPH' registered for protected routes
Token acquisition flow ready for interactive or silent auth
Protected endpoint /me returns user profile with Bearer token

best_practices: Always use MemoryStorage only for development. Switch to BlobStorage or CosmosDB for production deployments to ensure data persistence.
Keep all secrets in environment variables using dotenv. Never commit credentials to source control.
Call end_stream() in finally blocks when implementing streaming responses to ensure proper connection cleanup.
anti_patterns: Do not use the old microsoft.agents import syntax. Always use microsoft_agents with underscores for current SDK versions.
Avoid hardcoding client IDs, secrets, or tenant IDs directly in source code. Use environment variables exclusively.
Do not skip JWT authorization middleware in production. Always include jwt_authorization_middleware in aiohttp Application configuration.

faq.question: What Python version is required for the Microsoft Agents SDK?
faq.answer: The Microsoft Agents SDK for Python requires Python 3.8 or higher. Python 3.10+ is recommended for best compatibility with all features.

faq.question: How do I register my agent application in Azure AD?
faq.answer: Go to Azure Portal > Azure Active Directory > App registrations. Create a new registration, note the Application (client) ID and Tenant ID. Add redirect URIs for your agent endpoints.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Microsoft Teams only?
faq.answer: Yes. While the SDK supports multiple channels, you can configure your agent specifically for Teams by setting the appropriate activity handlers and Teams-specific middleware.

faq.question: What is the difference between AgentApplication and CloudAdapter?
faq.answer: CloudAdapter handles HTTP request/response cycles and message routing. AgentApplication manages conversation state, message handlers, and application-level logic. They work together in the hosting pipeline.

faq.question: How do I enable streaming responses in my agent?
faq.answer: Use context.streaming_response methods: set_feedback_loop(True), set_generated_by_ai_label(True), queue_text_chunk() for incremental updates, and end_stream() to finalize.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is designed for use with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. All prompt templates and code examples work across these AI assistant platforms.
